info.title: UseZombie Control Plane API
info.description: Zombie automation control plane — webhook-driven agent execution in a bwrap+landlock sandbox.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: System status and readiness probes

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: CLI login flow via device-code style sessions and GitHub OAuth

tags.name: Workspaces
tags.description: Create and manage workspaces (one workspace per repo)

tags.name: Activity
tags.description: Workspace-wide activity feed (scheduled spec queues, zombie state transitions, webhook ingestion)

tags.name: Runs
tags.description: Core spec-to-PR pipeline — queue, monitor, stream, retry, and cancel runs

tags.name: Zombies
tags.description: Zombie management and webhook ingestion — create, configure, and operate event-driven Zombies

tags.name: Memory
tags.description: Persistent zombie memory — store, recall, list, and forget key-value entries scoped to a zombie

tags.name: Credentials
tags.description: BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) LLM provider credentials

tags.name: Skill Secrets
tags.description: Per-skill secret storage for agent skill integrations

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Platform-wide configuration — manage shared LLM provider keys

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Plan upgrades and billing events

tags.name: Integration Grants
tags.description: Zombie runtime permission grants for third-party services (Slack, GitHub, etc.)

tags.name: Agent Keys
tags.description: Per-zombie API keys for LangGraph/CrewAI/Composio agents to call the Execute endpoint. Workspace-scoped.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Tenant-level admin API keys (`zmb_t_` prefix). Rotatable, self-service, replace the legacy `API_KEY` env var for admin auth.

tags.name: Execute
tags.description: Synchronous tool execution proxy for external agents

tags.name: Slack
tags.description: Slack OAuth install flow and inbound events/interactions webhooks

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Approval-gate webhooks invoked by humans to unblock a paused zombie

tags.name: Telemetry
tags.description: Per-zombie execution telemetry and internal operator telemetry

tags.name: Spec
tags.description: Spec templating and preview endpoints for the agent relay

tags.name: Scoring
tags.description: Per-workspace scoring configuration

get.description: Returns 200 as long as the process is alive and able to serve HTTP. Does not probe dependencies — use /readyz for readiness (database + queue).

commit.description: Git commit SHA of the running build

get.description: Admin-only platform health snapshot — in-flight request counts, queue depth, error rates. Requires the admin API key, not a user bearer token.
Returns Prometheus-format metrics for scraping by monitoring infrastructure.
Kubernetes-style readiness probe. Returns 200 when both the database and the Redis queue are reachable; returns 503 fail-closed if either dependency is degraded.

503.description: At least one dependency is degraded; ready=false and the failing dependency's boolean is false

get.description: Lists all configured platform-wide LLM provider keys. These are shared keys that workspaces fall back to when no BYOK key is configured.

put.description: Configures a platform-wide LLM provider key by referencing a workspace that has a BYOK key stored. The platform key proxies through the referenced workspace's credential.

source_workspace_id.description: Workspace whose BYOK key to use as the platform key

delete.description: Removes a platform-wide LLM provider key. Workspaces without BYOK keys will no longer be able to use this provider.

post.description: Creates a device-code style auth session. Returns a session ID and a login URL to present to the user in their browser.

login_url.description: URL to open in the user's browser to complete authentication

get.description: Polls a pending auth session. The CLI calls this repeatedly until status transitions to 'complete' (token available) or 'expired'.

token.description: Bearer token, present only when status is 'complete'

post.description: Called by the web frontend after successful OAuth to attach a token to the pending session. The CLI's next poll will receive the token.

post.summary: Execute a tool on behalf of an external agent
post.description: Synchronous tool execution proxy. The external agent calls this endpoint with its API key (bearer), names a tool, and receives the tool's result inline. Used by LangGraph / CrewAI / Composio integrations.

target.description: Target URL for the outbound call.

method.description: HTTP method to use against the target.

credential_ref.description: Named credential from the workspace vault to inject on the outbound request.

body.description: Request body to forward to the target. Null for GET/DELETE.

200.description: Tool executed; upstream status and body are returned plus UseZombie metadata.

status.description: HTTP status returned by the upstream target.

body.description: Response body returned by the upstream target (as string).

get.description: Handles the GitHub OAuth redirect. Exchanges the authorization code for an access token and redirects the user back to the app.

302.description: Redirect to application after successful OAuth exchange

post.summary: Forget a memory entry by key
post.description: Deletes the entry with the given `key` under the zombie instance. Returns `{ deleted: false }` if no match — never errors on miss.

get.description: Returns most-recent entries, optionally filtered by category.

get.summary: Recall memory entries matching a query
get.description: Case-insensitive LIKE search across both `key` and `content`. LIKE metacharacters in `query` are escaped — a query of `%` does NOT return every entry.

post.summary: Store or upsert a memory entry
post.description: Writes a `(key, content, category)` entry scoped to a zombie instance. Idempotent upsert on `(key, instance_id)`.

get.description: Exchanges the OAuth code for a team access token and persists team→workspace binding. Validates the HMAC-signed state nonce.

302.description: Redirect to the post-install page in the UseZombie dashboard.

post.description: Entry point for Slack Events API deliveries (message, app_mention, etc.). Verified via the `X-Slack-Signature` HMAC header; all other auth headers are ignored. Returns 200 immediately to satisfy Slack's 3s ack budget.

get.description: Redirects the user to Slack's OAuth consent screen. Used by the workspace owner to install the UseZombie Slack app.

302.description: Redirect to Slack OAuth consent URL.

post.description: Entry point for Slack Block Kit / shortcut / modal submissions. Verified via the `X-Slack-Signature` HMAC header. Form-encoded body per Slack's spec.

Ingress endpoint for Svix-signed webhooks (e.g. Clerk user lifecycle events). Signature is verified by the webhook auth middleware against the workspace's Svix secret before the handler runs. Body shape is event-specific and defined by the upstream issuer; the server validates the envelope and dispatches to the zombie's event log.

parameters.name: zombie_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the zombie receiving the webhook.

parameters.name: svix-id
parameters.description: Svix message identifier (used for deduplication and signature binding).

parameters.name: svix-timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of the Svix delivery (used for replay protection).

parameters.name: svix-signature
parameters.description: Space-separated list of Svix signatures (v1 scheme).

schema.description: Opaque event envelope; upstream issuer (Svix) defines the payload shape per event type.

202.description: Webhook accepted and queued for processing.

post.summary: Deliver an inbound webhook event to a Zombie
post.description: Delivers an inbound event to the Zombie identified by `zombie_id`. The Zombie is looked up by primary key — no JSONB traversal, no source-name uniqueness required. Bearer token is validated against the Zombie's `trigger.token` (omit if no token configured). Duplicate events (same `event_id` within 24 h) return 200 `{status: "duplicate"}`. A new event is enqueued to the Zombie's Redis stream and returns 202.

parameters.name: zombie_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the target Zombie. Obtained from `POST /v1/zombies` or `zombiectl up`.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event. Used for idempotency deduplication (24-hour window).

data.description: Event payload — any JSON object. Passed as-is into the Zombie event loop.

200.description: Duplicate event — already processed within the 24-hour dedup window.

202.description: Event accepted and enqueued for the Zombie.

404.description: No active Zombie is configured for this source (UZ-WH-001).

409.description: Zombie exists but is paused or stopped (UZ-WH-003).

post.description: Called by a human (via email link or Slack action) to approve or reject a paused zombie. Body is HMAC-signed by the issuer; the signature is verified against the zombie's webhook secret.

post.summary: Approve or reject an integration grant request
post.description: Human-in-the-loop approval webhook for integration grant requests (Slack, GitHub, etc.). HMAC-signed.
Creates a new workspace linked to a GitHub repository. Returns the workspace ID and a GitHub App install URL if the app is not yet installed on the repo.

default_branch.description: Default branch name, defaults to 'main' if omitted

install_url.description: GitHub App installation URL — direct user here if the app is not yet installed

get.description: Workspace-wide merged activity stream across every zombie. Cursor-paginated newest-first. Backs the dashboard Recent Activity feed.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call. Omit for the first page.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for the next page. Null when the feed is exhausted.

post.description: Records a billing lifecycle event such as a payment failure or downgrade. The workspace's billing status is updated accordingly.
Upgrades a workspace's billing plan by linking a payment provider subscription. The workspace's plan tier and billing status are updated immediately.

plan_tier.description: New plan tier (e.g. free, pro, enterprise)

get.description: Returns a billing breakdown for a workspace: run counts grouped by lifecycle event (completed, non-billable, score-gated), plus total agent seconds and average score for gated runs. Filterable by period.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Time period for the summary window

parameters.name: period_days
parameters.description: Integer form of the summary window. Takes precedence over the legacy `period` param.

total_cents.description: Sum of credit_deducted_cents across the window.

get.summary: List credentials stored for a workspace
get.description: Returns credential names. Values are never returned — they live encrypted in `vault.secrets`.

post.summary: Store a credential in the workspace vault
post.description: Envelope-encrypted with the KEK (master key v1) and persisted to `vault.secrets`. The raw value is never stored unencrypted and never returned.

put.description: Stores or replaces the BYOK LLM API key for a workspace. The key is encrypted at rest and never returned in API responses.

api_key.description: API key for the provider — encrypted at rest, never echoed

get.description: Returns whether a BYOK LLM key is configured for the workspace and which provider it belongs to. Never returns the actual key.

has_key.description: True if an API key is stored

delete.description: Removes the stored BYOK LLM API key for the workspace. Future runs will fall back to platform-provided keys if available.

post.description: Creates a new `zmb_t_`-prefixed tenant admin key. **The raw key is returned once.** Only the SHA-256 hash is persisted — the key cannot be retrieved again. Requires operator (or admin) role.

key_name.description: Unique per tenant. Alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores only.

key.description: Raw `zmb_t_` tenant key (70 chars). Shown once — store securely.

409.description: Key name already exists for this tenant (`UZ-APIKEY-005`).

get.description: Returns metadata for every tenant API key visible to the caller. Results are always scoped to the caller's tenant; `key_hash` is never returned.

total.description: Total rows across all pages for this tenant.

patch.description: Partial lifecycle update. Body must be `{"active": false}` — re-activation is not supported; mint a new key instead. Sets `revoked_at` and `updated_at`.

409.description: Key is already revoked (`UZ-APIKEY-006`) or attempted to set `active: true` (`UZ-APIKEY-007`).

delete.summary: Delete a revoked tenant API key
delete.description: Hard-deletes the row from `core.api_keys`. The key must already be revoked (`active = false`); an active key returns `409 UZ-APIKEY-008`.

409.description: Active key must be revoked before deletion (`UZ-APIKEY-008`).

get.summary: List agent keys in a workspace
get.description: Returns agent-key metadata only — never returns `key_hash`.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

post.summary: Issue an agent key for a zombie
post.description: Creates a new `zmb_` agent key bound to a specific zombie. **The raw key is returned once.** Only the SHA-256 hash is persisted — the key cannot be retrieved again.

key.description: Raw `zmb_` API key. Shown once — store securely.

delete.description: Revokes the agent key immediately. Subsequent requests with the key receive 401.

post.description: Persists the scoring thresholds and weights for run evaluation in this workspace.

scoring_context_max_tokens.description: Maximum token budget for the scoring prompt context.

get.description: Returns all zombies the caller has access to in the given workspace. Response envelope follows the RAD §8 `items`/`total` convention.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

post.description: Creates a new zombie from a SKILL.md source and a config JSON. The CLI (`zombiectl up`) compiles TRIGGER.md frontmatter into JSON before upload; the server never sees raw YAML.

config_json.description: Zombie config serialised as a JSON string.

delete.description: Marks the zombie as killed. Idempotent: re-killing a killed zombie returns the same 200 response. Scoped to workspace — cross-workspace deletes are rejected at the SQL layer as defence-in-depth.

get.description: Returns the append-only activity stream for a zombie (event received, skill invoked, response returned, credential resolved, grant requested, etc.). Cursor-based pagination.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from the previous response.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. null when no more items.

get.summary: Get billing summary for a single zombie
get.description: Per-zombie slice of the workspace billing summary. Response schema matches the workspace-scope endpoint plus a `zombie_id` field. SDK shape: `workspaces.billing.summary(ws)` vs `workspaces.zombies.billing.summary(ws, zombie)`.

parameters.name: period_days
parameters.description: Time window in days. Matches the workspace-scope billing summary endpoint.

get.summary: List integration grants for a zombie
get.description: Returns pending, approved, and revoked grants, newest first.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

delete.description: Immediately revokes a grant. A revoked grant surfaces `UZ-GRANT-003` to the zombie on its next execute attempt against the affected service.

parameters.name: grant_id
parameters.description: Opaque grant ID returned by integration-requests.

post.description: Called by a zombie (or on its behalf) to request permission to use a third-party integration (e.g. Slack, GitHub). Creates a pending grant that a human approves via the grant-approval webhook.

get.description: Returns execution telemetry for a specific zombie — last activity, event count, budget consumed, error counts.

schema.description: Telemetry payload (shape evolves with operator needs).

post.description: Send a message to a running zombie to nudge it at the next gate checkpoint. Queued (next checkpoint) or instant (IPC delivery) per body.

Transitions a zombie's status from `active` or `paused` to `stopped` and records a `zombie_stopped` activity event. Returns 409 if the zombie is already stopped or killed. Returns 404 if the zombie is not in the path workspace (cross-workspace IDOR guard).
Toggles the paused state of a workspace. When paused, no new runs will be started and queued runs will be held. Uses optimistic concurrency via the version field.

pause.description: True to pause, false to unpause

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the state change

version.description: Optimistic concurrency version — must match current workspace version

post.description: Reads PENDING_*.md spec files from the repository's default branch and upserts them into the control plane. Returns the count of newly synced specs.

synced_count.description: Number of specs synced in this request

BearerAuth.description: Obtain a token via the CLI auth flow (POST /v1/auth/sessions) or GitHub OAuth

docs_uri.description: Stable link to documentation for this error code

title.description: Short human-readable label, same for every occurrence of this code

detail.description: Instance-specific context describing what went wrong
detail.example: No zombie with id 'abc123' in this workspace.

ReadyzBody.description: Response body for GET /readyz. Identical shape for both 200 (healthy) and 503 (degraded) responses — only the boolean values differ.

ready.description: Overall readiness — true only when every dependency is healthy. Mirrors the HTTP status (true ↔ 200, false ↔ 503).

database.description: True if the PostgreSQL connection pool is reachable and a trivial query succeeds

queue.description: True if the Redis queue backend is reachable

PlatformKey.description: A platform-wide LLM provider key configuration

source_workspace_id.description: Workspace whose BYOK key is used

active.description: Whether this platform key is active

TenantApiKey.description: Metadata for a tenant API key. `key_hash` and the raw key are never returned.

last_used_at.description: Stays null until the async-stamping workstream ships.

file_path.description: Path to the spec file in the repository

title.description: Spec title extracted from the document

AgentProfile.description: Agent profile including trust and improvement metadata

trust_level.description: Current trust level (e.g. low, medium, high)

trust_streak_runs.description: Number of consecutive successful runs contributing to trust

improvement_stalled_warning.description: True if the agent's self-improvement has plateaued

AgentRelayRequest.description: Request body for Agent Relay LLM endpoints (spec template and preview). The workspace_id is provided via the path parameter.

tools.description: Optional tool definitions for the LLM to use during generation

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input

Artifact.description: An artifact produced during a run (e.g. plan, diff, review)

checksum_sha256.description: SHA-256 checksum of the artifact content

producer.description: Pipeline stage that produced the artifact

attempt.description: Run attempt number that produced this artifact

PolicyEvent.description: A policy evaluation event from the Warden stage

action_class.description: Classification of the action being evaluated

rule_id.description: Identifier of the policy rule that matched

actor.description: Pipeline stage that triggered the policy check

Proposal.description: An agent self-tuning proposal — a suggested change to the agent configuration

trigger_reason.description: Why the agent generated this proposal

proposed_changes.description: Description of the proposed configuration changes

approval_mode.description: Whether the proposal requires manual approval or can auto-apply

status.description: Proposal lifecycle status (e.g. PENDING, APPLIED, REJECTED, VETOED)

auto_apply_at.description: Timestamp when the proposal will auto-apply, null if manual approval required

Transition.description: A state transition in the run pipeline

actor.description: Pipeline stage or user that triggered the transition
